I have completed the end-to-end analysis and report.
• Parses all relevant JSON files under data/.
• Matches experimental counts with ideal probabilities.
• Computes an XEB-like fidelity estimator per (N, d, r) with bootstrap
• Reads ideal bitstrings (lists) and converts to tuple keys.
• Computes ideal-bitstring match fraction per (N, d, r).
• Generates figures and saves them under report/images/.
• Columns: N, d, r, f_xeb, f_xeb_std, nsamples.
• Columns: N, d, r, match_frac, total_shots.
• Mean XEB-like fidelity vs circuit depth for N = 40, with error bars
• Ideal-bitstring match fraction vs number of qubits N for several
• Introduction to the RCS context and goals.
• Construction of XEB-like fidelity from partial amplitude subsets.
• Ideal-bitstring match fraction metric for transport circuits.
• Trends of XEB-like fidelity vs depth for N = 40 (Figure 1).
• Trends of match fraction vs N and vs depth for transport circuits
• Interpretation in terms of noise accumulation and fidelity decay.
• Relation of observed fidelities to classical approximability and
lack of explicit noise model, no explicit classical benchmarks).
• Conclusion summarizing how the observed fidelity trends support the
existence of a gap between experimental performance and classical
